steps.title: Recognize the passage structure
steps.content: The passage moves through four specific elements: a major award (1932 Wanamaker), a prestigious orchestral performance (Chicago Symphony), her compositional contributions, and recent major orchestra recordings—a progression of achievement and recognition.

steps.title: Match the stated purpose
steps.content:
A says the text “provides examples of Price's importance as a composer,” which is exactly how the passage works: each fact accumulates to demonstrate her stature as a composer.
  • B falsely claims the passage argues for more performances
  • C wrongly emphasizes her musical style
  • D fabricates a comparison between composers
.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
